div.h2: Why eBPF Latency Measurement Requires Monotonic Time

ul.li: No Regressions: T(n) >= T(n-1) always holds.
NTP Immune: NTP can adjust frequency (slewing) but never steps the counter.
Execution Linear: Reflects actual CPU/System time passed since boot.

g.title: The standard helper for eBPF timing

div.p: Wall-clock time is for user-facing timestamps (the "When"). Monotonic time is for measuring intervals (the "Duration"). In eBPF observability, using bpf_ktime_get_ns() is mandatory to prevent statistical corruption from NTP adjustments.

ul.li: Error: NTP step back corrupted interval.

div.p: If NTP steps the clock backward during a syscall:

ul.li: Overhead: Extremely low (reads VDSO or system register).

g.title: The standard helper for eBPF timing
